fix(ci): pin dorny/test-reporter to v1.9.1 and add continue-on-error

With FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true the runner forces
dorny/test-reporter@v1 (built for Node 16/20) onto Node 24. This breaks
the action HTTP client internals causing it to successfully CREATE the
check run but fail with HttpError: Not Found on the subsequent UPDATE
call. The failure is intermittent (race condition on concurrent jobs)
which is why it only hit server-debug on this run.

Fixes:
  1. Pin to v1.9.1 (ships Node 20 support, less sensitive to the forced
     Node 24 upgrade)
  2. continue-on-error: true on all four reporter steps -- a reporting
     glitch should never fail a CI run whose tests actually passed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): BDD step exits 1 despite passing

Root cause (reading actual CI logs):
  Cucumber passed (18/18 server, 4/4 client) but the step exited 1.
  After cucumber finishes the wire server process exits on its own.
  The subsequent process termination call then targets a non-existent
  PID, returning exit code 1. With shell: sh -e {0} this aborts the
  script before the RESULT exit, reporting failure despite passing tests.

Fix: add || true to the process cleanup (both server-ci and client-ci)

Also included (defensive):
  - BUNDLE_SILENCE_ROOT_WARNING -> BUNDLE_ALLOW_ROOT=1 (correct Bundler 2.x var)
  - Add x86_64-linux to both Gemfile.lock PLATFORMS sections
    (lockfiles were aarch64-only; regenerated via docker so BUNDLED WITH
    stays 2.4.20 matching the builder image)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore original ISO volume label in inject-iso-branding.sh

xorriso -report_el_torito reports El Torito boot entries and never
outputs --volid, so the grep always failed and the || fallback fired,
setting the branded ISO label to INFERNO-INSTALL.

The grub.cfg baked by bootc-image-builder uses:
  inst.stage2=hd:LABEL=Fedora-S-dvd-x86_64-43

With label INFERNO-INSTALL, dracut could not find the installer root
device and hung at devexists /dev/root indefinitely (v12 boot failure).

Fix: use xorriso -indev to read the actual volume ID from the original
ISO before repackaging. Fallback to Fedora-S-dvd-x86_64-43 if xorriso
output format changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: pre-warm Conan cache to avoid redundant source builds

Add a dedicated `conan-cache` job that runs once after `prepare`
and installs all Conan packages before the parallel build jobs start.

- All five Linux build jobs (tidy, server-debug, client-debug, server-ci,
  client-ci) now depend on `conan-cache` instead of `prepare`
- Unified all Linux debug cache keys to `conan-linux-debug-*` (previously
  debug jobs used `conan-debug-Linux-*` and ci jobs used `conan-ci-Linux-*`
  — both Debug+cppstd=23 — causing the same packages to be compiled twice
  on a cold cache)
- The cache job skips conan install entirely on a cache hit so subsequent
  commits are fast

First build still compiles from source (expected — no cache yet).
Every subsequent run with an unchanged conanfile.py restores the cache
and skips source compilation entirely.

  history/users events in ChatController instead of manual serialization,
  ensuring proper type discrimination for polymorphic ChatMessage types.

  matching the pattern used by other event types.

  (Resolved by mvn clean compile)

  to always attempt JSON parsing first, extract message/error fields from
  response body, fall back to generic 'Server error (status)' only when
  body is not JSON.

Bug 6: Wrong password allows private room entry - Add joinedRef tracking
  in useWebSocket; errors received before successful join (history/users)
  are treated as connection rejections. ChatPage navigates back to lobby
  on connection error.

fix: strip prompt echo from output and fix new-session detection

Bug 1 (prompt echo in live tab):
The Copilot SDK prepends <current_datetime> to assistant messages, and
gpt-5-mini sometimes echoes the instruction headers (Language:, Format:,
Task:) verbatim at the start of its response instead of only outputting
the translated content.

- Add StripTranslationOutput() in translator/prompt.go: strips XML tags
  (e.g. <current_datetime>) and leading prompt-header echoes starting
  with 'Language:' / '言語:' from AI output before display.
- Apply StripTranslationOutput() in buildReasoningLines() and
  buildHistoryBlock() in tui/viewer.go.
- Strengthen RTSystemPrompt() and HistSystemPrompt() to explicitly
  instruct the model not to reproduce instruction headers in output.
- Add unit tests for StripTranslationOutput covering English, Japanese,
  and combined echo patterns.

Bug 2 (new-session detection/alert not firing):
New sessions were first detected as *inactive* (lock file not yet
created) and immediately added to knownSessionIDs. When the lock file
was written shortly after, the session was already "known" so the
active-session alert was never shown.

- Fix updateKnownSessions() in tui/app.go: inactive new sessions are no
  longer added to knownSessionIDs on first sight. They are held out of
  the map until seen as active, allowing the alert to fire correctly on
  the state transition from inactive → active.

commit.message:
fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter (#307937)

* fix: exempt agent instructions from workspace subpath filter

AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md live at the workspace
root by design. The CLI harness restricts items to workspaceSubpaths
(.github, .copilot, .agents, .claude) which filtered out these root-level
agent instruction files. Now items with groupKey 'agent-instructions' are
exempt from the subpath filter, matching the existing exemption for
instructionFileFilter patterns.

* sessions: include AGENTS.md in instructions listing

The sessions AI customization tree view and overview were only calling
listPromptFiles(PromptsType.instructions) to discover instruction files.
However, AGENTS.md (along with CLAUDE.md and copilot-instructions.md)
is classified as an agent type by getPromptFileType(), so it was never
returned by that call.

Fix by also calling listAgentInstructions() and merging the results
(deduplicating by URI), matching the pattern already used by the
workbench customization editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
